name: Kinect Azure TOP
description: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows 10 April 2018 or newer operating system.
summary: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows 10 April 2018 or newer operating system.

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Enable or disable the camera.

parameters.name: Hardware Type
parameters.label: Hardware Type
parameters.description:
library -  - Choose whether you are using Microsoft Kinect Azure hardware or a Kinect compatible Orbbec camera (Femto Mega, Femto Bolt, etc). Both camera types can be used together in the same project, but only one hardware type can enable body tracking at any one time.

 microsoft - Select this option if you are using an original Microsoft Kinect Azure camera. orbbec - Selection this option for a Kinect compatible Orbbec camera e.g. Femto Mega, Femto Bolt, etc. Not all Orbbec cameras are compatible with the Kinect Azure. For general Orbbec support use an Orbbec TOP.

parameters.name: Microsoft Kinect Azure
parameters.label: Microsoft Kinect Azure
parameters.description: microsoft - Select this option if you are using an original Microsoft Kinect Azure camera. orbbec - Selection this option for a Kinect compatible Orbbec camera e.g. Femto Mega, Femto Bolt, etc. Not all Orbbec cameras are compatible with the Kinect Azure. For general Orbbec support use an Orbbec TOP.

parameters.name: Sensor
parameters.label: Sensor
parameters.description: sensor - The serial number of the connected Kinect Azure camera. The  will automatically fill the list with all available cameras. Note: Only one Kinect Azure  should be connected to a single camera.

parameters.name: Camera FPS
parameters.label: Camera FPS
parameters.description:
fps -  - Controls the frame rate of both the color and depth cameras. Some higher camera resolutions are not supported when running at 30FPS. Lower framerates can produce brighter color images in low light conditions.

 fps5 - fps15 - fps30 -

parameters.name: 5 FPS
parameters.label: 5 FPS
parameters.description: fps5 - fps15 - fps30 -

parameters.name: Color Resolution
parameters.label: Color Resolution
parameters.description:
colorres -  - The resolution of images captured by the color camera. Different resolutions may have different aspect ratios. Note: 4096 x 3072 is not supported at 30 .

 1280x720 - 1920x1080 - 2560x1440 - 2048x1536 - 3840x2160 - 4096x3072 - Note: This resolution is not supported when operating at 30FPS. Reduce the framerate to 15 or 5  to use this setting.

parameters.name: 1280 x 720 (16:9)
parameters.label: 1280 x 720 (16:9)
parameters.description: 1280x720 - 1920x1080 - 2560x1440 - 2048x1536 - 3840x2160 - 4096x3072 - Note: This resolution is not supported when operating at 30FPS. Reduce the framerate to 15 or 5  to use this setting.

parameters.name: Depth Mode
parameters.label: Depth Mode
parameters.description:
depthmode -  - The depth mode controls which of the Kinect's two depth cameras (Wide or Narrow FOV) are used to produce the depth image and whether any 'binning' is used to process the data. In 'binned' modes, 2x2 blocks of pixels are combined to produce a filter, lower resolution image. Note: Body tracking is not supported when using the Passive IR depth mode.

 narrowunbinned - widebinned - narrowbinned - Note: This mode is not recommended for use with body tracking. wideunbinned - Note: This mode is not supported at 30FPS and is not recommended for use with body tracking. passiveir - Note: This mode does not support body tracking.

parameters.name: Narrow FOV - Unbinned (640x576)
parameters.label: Narrow FOV - Unbinned (640x576)
parameters.description: narrowunbinned - widebinned - narrowbinned - Note: This mode is not recommended for use with body tracking. wideunbinned - Note: This mode is not supported at 30FPS and is not recommended for use with body tracking. passiveir - Note: This mode does not support body tracking.

parameters.name: Body Tracking Model
parameters.label: Body Tracking Model
parameters.description: modelpath - The file path to the onnx model that performs body tracking features. TouchDesigner includes the regular and lite models that are part of the Kinect Azure SDK.

parameters.name: Body Tracking Processing Mode
parameters.label: Body Tracking Processing Mode
parameters.description:
proccessingmode -  - Determines how the body tracking model is processed. The default mode runs mostly on the  (supports Nvidia, AMD and Intel), but this can also be switched to a CPU mode when a compatible  is not available.

 gpudefault - The default processing mode. It is equivalent to DirectML when running on Windows. cpu - The AI model will run on the CPU instead of the graphics cards. This is notably slower and not recommended for production release. directml - Equivalent to the default mode when running on Windows. The AI model will run on the . This is compatible with Nvidia, AMD and Intel GPUs.

parameters.name: GPU Default
parameters.label: GPU Default
parameters.description: gpudefault - The default processing mode. It is equivalent to DirectML when running on Windows. cpu - The AI model will run on the CPU instead of the graphics cards. This is notably slower and not recommended for production release. directml - Equivalent to the default mode when running on Windows. The AI model will run on the . This is compatible with Nvidia, AMD and Intel GPUs.

parameters.name: Body Tracking GPU Device
parameters.label: Body Tracking GPU Device
parameters.description: gpu - The device number of the  to use when there are multiple GPUs in the system. The ordering system may be dependent on the  manufacturer.

parameters.name: Sensor Orientation
parameters.label: Sensor Orientation
parameters.description:
orientation -  - Used to indicate when the camera is mounted in a non-upright position. This can help improve body-tracking results.

 default - cw90 - ccw90 - flipped -

parameters.name: Default
parameters.label: Default
parameters.description: default - cw90 - ccw90 - flipped -

parameters.name: Image
parameters.label: Image
parameters.description: A list of available image types to capture from the device and display in this .

parameters.name: Color
parameters.label: Color
parameters.description: bit RGBA image from the color camera.

parameters.name: Align Image to Other Camera
parameters.label: Align Image to Other Camera
parameters.description:
remapimage - When enabled, the current image will be remapped to align with images from the other camera. For example, use this feature to create a color camera image that maps to the pixels of the depth camera.
The current depth mode and color resolution will be used to do the remapping.
Note: Remapping the point cloud image can create artifacts in the distribution of points due to gaps in the remapping algorithm.

parameters.name: Sync Image to Body Tracking
parameters.label: Sync Image to Body Tracking
parameters.description: bodyimage - When enabled, the image produced will be delayed so that it corresponds to the most recent data in the body tracking system. The amount of delay may fluctuate based on the power of the processor doing the body tracking and the complexity of the scene. A Kinect Azure Select TOP can be used at the same time to retrieve the real-time image stream.

parameters.name: Mirror Image
parameters.label: Mirror Image
parameters.description: mirrorimage - Flip the image in the horizontal axis.

parameters.name: CPU Body Tracking
parameters.label: CPU Body Tracking
parameters.description: cpu - When enabled, body tracking calculations will be done on the CPU rather than on the graphics card. This method is much slower, but does not require a high-powered graphics card to function.

parameters.name: Reset Color Controls
parameters.label: Reset Color Controls
parameters.description: resetcolors - Reset all of the color controls to the camera's defaults. These vary per camera and may be different than the parameter defaults.

parameters.name: Enable Color Controls
parameters.label: Enable Color Controls
parameters.description: enablecolors - Turn on to enable adjustment controls for the color camera. When disabled, the previous color settings will remain in place. Use the Reset Color Controls button to switch the camera back to its default settings.

parameters.name: Manual Exposure
parameters.label: Manual Exposure
parameters.description:
manualexposure - Enable to allow setting the exposure time manually. When disabled, the camera will automatically choose an exposure based on the light levels and frame rate.
Note: This feature may not work correctly due to issues in the current Kinect SDK.

parameters.name: Exposure Time
parameters.label: Exposure Time
parameters.description:
exposure - Adjust the exposure time of the color image measured in microseconds. The time must be less than one frame.
Note: This feature may not work correctly due to issues in the current Kinect SDK.

parameters.name: Manual White Balance
parameters.label: Manual White Balance
parameters.description: manualwhitebalance - Enable to allow setting the camera white balance manually.

parameters.name: White Balance
parameters.label: White Balance
parameters.description: whitebalance - Select the temperature in degrees Kelvin used to set the white balance of the image. The value is rounded to the nearest 10 degrees.

parameters.name: Brightness
parameters.label: Brightness
parameters.description: brightness - Used to adjust the brightness of the image from 0 to 255. 128 is the default.

parameters.name: Contrast
parameters.label: Contrast
parameters.description: contrast - Conrtols the contrast of the color image.

parameters.name: Saturation
parameters.label: Saturation
parameters.description: saturation - Controls the saturation of the color image.

parameters.name: Sharpness
parameters.label: Sharpness
parameters.description: sharpness - Adjusts the sharpness of the color image.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.label: Gain
parameters.description: gain - The gain of the color image.

parameters.name: Backlight Compensation
parameters.label: Backlight Compensation
parameters.description: backlight - Enables compensation for bright back lighting in a scene.

parameters.name: Powerline Frequency
parameters.label: Powerline Frequency
parameters.description:
powerfreq -  - Select the frequency of the power supply for use in the cameras noise cancellation system.

 50hz - 60hz -

parameters.name: 50Hz
parameters.label: 50Hz
parameters.description: 50hz - 60hz -

parameters.name: Depth Image Delay
parameters.label: Depth Image Delay
parameters.description: depthdelay - A delay in microseconds between when the depth and color images are captured. The delay must be less than one frame in length based on the current framerate.

parameters.name: Wired Sync Mode
parameters.label: Wired Sync Mode
parameters.description:
syncmode -  - When using more than one Kinect Azure camera, this setting can be used to determine which unit is the master and which are subordinates.

 standalone - master - subordinate -

parameters.name: Standalone
parameters.label: Standalone
parameters.description: standalone - master - subordinate -

parameters.name: Subordinate Delay
parameters.label: Subordinate Delay
parameters.description: subdelay - A delay in microseconds between when the master unit captures an image and when this device captures an image. (Only applicable for subordinate devices).

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail. When the input is 32-bit float format, only nearest filtering will be used (regardless of what is selected).

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. For every pass after the first it takes the result of the previous pass and replaces the node's first input with the result of the previous pass. One exception to this is the GLSL TOP when using compute shaders, where the input will continue to be the connected 's image.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
